# Principles of War and Peace

**Principle 1: Total War Has No Limits. The Pacific War was savage on both sides.**

**Principle 2: The Bomb Was a Decision. Not inevitable. Truman could have chosen invasion.**

**Principle 3: Victory Requires Sacrifice. The cost of victory was staggering in lives.**

**Case: The Kamikaze. Japanese pilots deliberately crashed into American ships at Okinawa.**

**Case: The Bataan Death March. Japanese treatment of POWs was brutal throughout the war.**

**Case: The Potsdam Conference. Truman learned of the successful bomb test at Potsdam.**

**Case: The No Surrender Order. Japanese soldiers were ordered to fight to the death. Surrender was worse than death.**
**Case: The Hiroshima Bombing Mission. The crew of the Enola Gay did not know what they were dropping. They thought it was a conventional bomb.**
**Case: The Nagasaki Bomb. The second bomb was originally scheduled for August 11. Weather moved it up to August 9.**
**Case: The Emperor's Broadcast. Hirohito's surrender broadcast was the first time most Japanese heard his voice.**
**Case: The Holdouts. Some Japanese soldiers continued fighting for years after the war.**
**Case: The Invasion Fear. Japanese civilians were being trained to fight with bamboo spears. The invasion would have been catastrophic.**
**Case: The US Casualties. The US had already suffered 400,000 casualties in the Pacific. The bomb prevented more.**
